content:
Match List - I with List - II

List - I
(Mixtures/Sample)		 List - II
(Technique used for purification)
(A) Glycerol from spent lye (I) Steam distillation
(B) Chloroform + Aniline (II) Fractional distillation
(C) Fractions of crude oil (III) Distillation under reduced pressure
(D) Aniline + Water (IV) Distillation

Choose the correct answer from the options given below :
explanation:
(A) Glycerol from spent lye $$\to$$ Distillation under reduced pressure

(B) Chloroform + Aniline $$\to$$ Distillation

(C) Fractions of crude oil $$\to$$ Fractional distillation

(D) Aniline + H$$_2$$O $$\to$$ Steam distillation

content:
Read the following statements and choose the set of correct statements :

(A) Chrome steel is used for cutting tools and crushing machines.

(B) The fine dust of aluminium is used in paints and lacquers.

(C) Copper is used for reduction of alcohol

(D) Zinc dust is used as a reducing agent in the manufacture of paints

(E) Iron is used for galvanising zinc

Choose the most appropriate answer from the options given below :
explanation:
Let's evaluate each statement for its correctness :

  • Statement (A): Chrome steel is used for cutting tools and crushing machines. This statement is correct. Chrome steel, also known as stainless steel when combined with nickel, is known for its hardness and resistance to corrosion, making it suitable for cutting tools and heavy machinery.


  • Statement (B): The fine dust of aluminium is used in paints and lacquers. This statement is correct. Aluminum powder is often used in paint and lacquer formulations to give them a metallic sheen.


  • Statement (C): Copper is used for the reduction of alcohol. This statement is incorrect. Copper is not typically used for the reduction of alcohol in chemical reactions. It's more commonly used in electrical wiring and plumbing due to its high electrical conductivity and malleability.


  • Statement (D): Zinc dust is used as a reducing agent in the manufacture of paints. This statement is correct. Zinc dust can act as a reducing agent in various chemical reactions, including in the paint manufacturing process.


  • Statement (E): Iron is used for galvanizing zinc. This statement is incorrect. It's actually the other way around; zinc is used for galvanizing iron or steel to protect it from corrosion.

Considering these evaluations, the correct answer is Option C: (A), (B) and (D) only. Statements (A), (B), and (D) are correct, while statements (C) and (E) are incorrect.

content: The reaction that does NOT take place in blast furnace between 900 K to 1500 K temperature range during extraction of iron is :
explanation:
Reaction

$$\mathrm{Fe}_{2} \mathrm{O}_{3}+\mathrm{CO} \rightarrow 2 \mathrm{FeO}+\mathrm{CO}_{2}$$

This reaction takes place at temperature (500 K - 800 K) not at (900 K to 1500 K)

content: The maximum temperature that can be achieved in blast furnace is :
explanation:
A blast furnace is generally used for reduction of iron oxides but it can be used for extraction of other metals like Pb from PbO, etc.

In a blast furnace, hot air is blown from the bottom of furnace. This bottom surface has the maximum temperature of upto 2200 K.

content: Which one of the following methods can be used to obtain highly pure metal which is liquid at room temperature?
explanation: Distillation method is generally used for the purification of metals having low boiling point such as Hg, Zn etc.

content: Identify the correct statement from the following :

options.content: Blister copper has blistered appearance due to evolution of CO<sub>2</sub>
Vapour phase refining is carried out for Nickel by Van Arkel method.
Pig iron can be moulded into a variety of shapes.
Wrought iron is impure iron with 4% carbon.

explanation: The iron obtained from blast furnace contains about 4% carbon and many impurities like S, P, Si, Mn in due to it's malleable nature they can smaller amount. This is known as pig iron and cast into variety of shapes.

content: Which one is malachite from the following?
explanation: Malachite = CuCO<sub>3</sub>.Cu(OH)<sub>2</sub>

content:
Considering Ellingham diagram, which of the
following metals can be used to reduce
alumina?
explanation:
Mg has more – $$\Delta $$G value than alumina. So
it will be in the lower part of Ellingham
diagram. Metals which have more – $$\Delta $$G
value can reduce those metal oxides which
have less – $$\Delta $$G value.

content: Extraction of gold and silver involves leaching with CN<sup>$$-$$</sup> ion. Silver  is later recovered by
explanation:
Zn being more reactive displaces Ag and Au.

4M(s) + 8NaCN(aq) + 2H2O(aq) + O2(g) $$ \to $$
          4Na[M(CN)2](aq) + OH(aq)

2Na[M(CN)2](aq) + Zn(s) $$ \to $$ Na2[Zn(CN)4](aq) +2M(s)

Here M = Ag or Au

This method is known as Mac-Arthur forest cyanide process.

content:
Match items of Column I with the items of Column II and assign the correct code :
Column $${\rm I}$$ Column $${\rm I}$$$${\rm I}$$
(A) Cyanide process (i) Ultrapure Ge
(B) Froth floatation process (ii) Dressing of ZnS
(C) Electrolytic reduction (iii) Extraction of Al
(D) Zone refining (iv) Extraction of Au
(v) Purification of Ni
explanation:
Cyanide process is used for the extraction of gold.

Froth floatation method is used for dressing of ZnS.

Aluminium is extracted by electrolytic reduction.

Zone refining is used for getting extra pure element like Ge.

content: In the extraction of copper from its sulphide ore. the metal is finally obtained by the reduction of cuprous oxide with
explanation: Cu<sub>2</sub>S + 2Cu<sub>2</sub>O $$ \to $$ 6Cu + SO<sub>2</sub> ↑

content:
''Metals are usually not found as nitrates in their ores.'' Out of the following two (I and II) reasons which is/are true for the above observation?
I.  Metal nitrates are highly unstable.
II.  Metal nitrates are highly soluble in water.

options.content: I is false but II is true.
I is true but II is false.

explanation:
All nitrates are soluble in water and are
quite stable as they do not decompose easily on
heating.

content: The metal oxide which cannot be reduced to metal by carbon is
explanation: Al<sub>2</sub>O<sub>3</sub> cannot be reduced by carbon.

content: Roasting of sulphides gives the gas X as a byproduct.  This is a colourless gas with choking smell of burnt sulphur and causes great damage to respiratory organs as a result of acid rain. Its aqueous solution is acidic, acts as a reducing agent and its acid has never been isolated. The gas X is
explanation:
SO2 gas is liberated when any sulphide ore is roasted.

2M2S + 3O2 $$ \to $$ 2M2O + 2SO2

SO2 is a colourless gas with chocking smell of burnt sulphur.

content: In the extraction of copper from its sulphide ore, the metal is finally obtained by the reduction of cuprous oxide with
explanation: Cu<sub>2</sub>S + 2Cu<sub>2</sub>O $$ \to $$ 6Cu + SO<sub>2</sub> ↑

content: Which one of the following is a mineral of iron?
explanation:
Magnetite is Fe<sub>3</sub>O<sub>4</sub>
 and contains upto
70% of the metal.

content: Aluminium is extracted from alumina (Al<sub>2</sub>O<sub>3</sub>) by electrolysis of a molten mixture of
explanation:
Alumina Al<sub>2</sub>O<sub>3</sub> before subjecting to electrolytes,
is mixed with fluorspar (CaF<sub>2</sub>) and cryolite
(Na<sub>3</sub>AlF<sub>6</sub>) which lower its melting point and make
it more conducting.

content: The following reactions take place in the blast furnace in the preparation of impure iron. Identify the reaction pertaining to the formation of the slag.
explanation:
Slag is formed by the reaction

CaO + SiO2 $$ \to $$ CaSiO3

content: Which of the following pairs of metals is purified by van-Arkel method?
explanation:
Van Arkel method is used for purification
of Zr and Ti.

content: Which of the following elements is present as the impurity to the maximum extent in the pig iron?
explanation:
Pig iron contains carbon (nearly 4%) as the
impurity to the maximum extent. Other impurities
are S, P, Si, Mn in trace amount.

content: Which of the following statements, about the advantage of roasting of sulphide ore before reduction is not true?

options.content: The $$\Delta $$G$$_f^o$$ of the sulphide is greater than those for CS<sub>2</sub> and H<sub>2</sub>S.
The $$\Delta $$G$$_f^o$$ is negative for roasting of sulphide ore to oxide.
Roasting of the sulphide to the oxide is thermodynamically feasible.
Carbon and hydrogen are suitable reducing agents for metal sulphides.

explanation:
Carbon and hydrogen are not suitable
reducing agents for metal sulphides.

content: Sulphide ores of metals are usually concentrated by froth floatation process. Which one of the following sulphide ores offer an exception and is concentrated by chemical leaching?
explanation:
Galena (PbS), copper pyrites (CuFeS<sub>2</sub>) and
argentite (Ag<sub>2</sub>S) are concentrated by froth floatation
process but sphalerite (ZnS) is concentrated by
chemical leaching.

content: The method of zone refining of metals is based on the principle of

options.content: greater mobility of the pure metal than that of the impurity
higher melting point of the impurity than that of the pure metal
greater noble character of the solid metal than that of the impurity
greater solubility of the impurity in the molten state than in the solid
